requiredChecks: slot selector stays a horizontal four-card strip
selected slot card visually outranks empty cards
focus bar stays a compact three-chip row below the slot strip
focused editor first-screen card stays dominant
preview and summary read as finished evaluative cards
save dock remains a full-width Signal Orange persistent CTA
settings stays a quiet gear action

notes: Top area stays compact: title, current roster context, settings access only.
Settings stays a quiet gear action in the top-right corner, not a large secondary button.
Render this as a horizontal four-card strip, not a vertical roster list.
The selected slot card must visually outrank the empty cards before the editor body starts.
Three compact chips sit directly under the slot strip and keep the active focus clearly highlighted.
Current active work area for frame, weapon, or mobility focus.
The first-screen editor card should already look finished: selected part title, strong primary stat, supporting stat row, and quiet auxiliary action.
Preview must look finished, not placeholder-like.
Use a framed blueprint viewer with a visible bottom meter so the surface still reads as evaluative even when model detail is sparse.
Summarize role, attack style, range, durability, and mobility outcome in short Korean labels.
Use compact stat rows or chips rather than loose placeholder copy.
Persistent primary CTA using Signal Orange.
Save dock is a full-width orange bottom bar with a lock icon and Korean label, stronger than any inline editor action.
Single scroll workspace, not fragmented dashboard panels.
Do not translate this screen into a vertical roster list plus small footer button.
